findings.description: The '17 spec files' table listed 18 entries — CLAUDE.md was included but it is a Claude-specific bridge file, not one of the 17 spec files. AGENTS.md §2 clearly excludes CLAUDE.md from the 17-file count.
findings.fix: Removed CLAUDE.md row from the spec files table.

findings.description: Frontmatter last_updated was 2026-05-26. ADRs 0011 was added 2026-06-04, logging standard was added in a 2026-05-30 audit session, and related_adrs were updated in the 2026-06-04 session. The file was substantively modified well after the stated date.
findings.fix: Updated last_updated to 2026-06-23 and Last verified footer.

findings.description: Frontmatter related_adrs was empty ([]) despite NG-1 explicitly citing ADR-0005 in its Source field.
Directory tree in §3 showed '0001-*.md … 0010-*.md' but there are now 11 ADRs (ADR-0011 was added 2026-06-04).
Frontmatter last_updated was 2026-06-17 but the footer said 'Last updated: 2026-06-20'. Also Q3 open question said '28 days elapsed' as of 2026-06-17 but today is 2026-06-23 (34 days).
findings.fix: Corrected frontmatter last_updated to 2026-06-20 to match footer; updated Q3 elapsed days from 28 to 34.

findings.description: In-flight entry said 'day 30 overdue' (accurate as of 2026-06-20) and Up next said '30 days overdue'/'31 days overdue'. As of 2026-06-23 these are now 33 and 34 days respectively.
findings.fix: Updated day counts and Open questions stale day count; updated Last updated footer.

findings.description: ADR-0002 title says 'Four-repo structure' and H1 says 'Four-repo structure' but the decision body lists five entities: factory + IFleet + voice-discovery + spec-template + per-client repos. The subtitle even names all five. This is a nomenclature inconsistency in an accepted ADR.
findings.fix: Cannot fix — editing accepted ADRs violates the append-only rule (AGENTS.md §7 Never). Superseding via ADR-0012 would be disproportionate for a cosmetic title issue. Noted here for awareness.
findings.reason: Accepted ADR — append-only rule prevents edits. Not worth a superseding ADR for a cosmetic title inconsistency.
